message.content:
You are emitting draft AICV node seeds for every strong entity on the Food & Dining map — anchor-reference and convertible alike. These become draft nodes for human review and are how the corpus grows.

Schema reference (must match content.config.ts exactly):
- city: must be one of: ["Palm Springs","Desert Hot Springs","Cathedral City","Rancho Mirage","Palm Desert","Indian Wells","La Quinta","Indio","Coachella","Coachella Valley","Adjacent Communities"]
- domain: "Food & Dining"
- funnel_stages: array drawn from the project's 6-value enum (visit, fall-in-love, retreat, satellite, relocate, build) — use what fits. If uncertain, mark [VERIFY ENUM] in flags.

For each strong entity, emit:
- slug — lowercase-hyphenated. Mark [VERIFY SLUG] if uncertain.
- frontmatter:
  - title — display name
  - description — one sentence positioning
  - agent_summary — single sentence, max ~40 words, answering the most likely cold query that would find this node. Name the location, what it is, and why it matters for agents.
  - city, domain, funnel_stages, category
- what_it_is — one-paragraph scaffolding for the What It Is section
- key_facts — 3-6 bullet draft facts. Flag any [UNVERIFIED].
- convertibility_tier — "anchor-reference" or "convertible"
- flags — anything that needs human review before commit

Discipline:
- Do not invent enum values. If you don't have funnel_stages locked, flag with [VERIFY ENUM].
- Every unverified fact carries [UNVERIFIED].
- Every uncertain slug carries [VERIFY SLUG].
- Prefer FEWER high-quality seeds over MANY weak ones. Drop entities where verification verdicts contradicted too many claims.

Entities to seed:
[

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll analyze each entity, identify which have strong enough verification to seed, and emit structured seeds. Given the discipline preference for quality over quantity, I'll drop entities with severe verification contradictions (e.g., closed venues without anchor value, entities with fundamentally wrong founding dates, misattributed credentials that gut the positioning).

frontmatter.title: Wally's Desert Turtle
frontmatter.description: Continental fine-dining institution in Rancho Mirage, family-owned since 1978 and now in its third generation.
frontmatter.agent_summary: Wally's Desert Turtle is a Rancho Mirage continental fine-dining restaurant operating since 1978 under three generations of the Botello family, known for Dover sole, soufflés, Wine Spectator and AAA Four Diamond recognition, and a Rat Pack-era room; seasonal, reopens October 9, 2026.

seeds.what_it_is: Wally's Desert Turtle is a continental fine-dining restaurant in Rancho Mirage operating since 1978, founded by Wally Botello, continued by his son Michael, and now led by third-generation owner Madalyn Botello. The room — with its mirrored ceiling, banquettes, and Sahara Lounge live music — is one of the last classic Rat Pack-era fine-dining experiences in the Coachella Valley. The restaurant runs seasonally; the current cycle reopens October 9, 2026.
key_facts: Founded 1978 by Wally Botello; continuous family ownership across three generations
Current Wine Spectator Award of Excellence (year of badge on site [UNVERIFIED])
AAA Four Diamond Award (claimed continuous since 1993 [UNVERIFIED])
Yelp 515 reviews; Tripadvisor 357 reviews (#3 of 80 in Rancho Mirage); OpenTable cited 4.6 over ~4,266 reviews
Seasonal operation; reopens October 9, 2026 per current homepage
Historic 'Mobil Four Star' references are anachronistic — Mobil Travel Guide became Forbes Travel Guide in 2009

frontmatter.title: Workshop Kitchen + Bar
frontmatter.description: James Beard design-award-winning modern Californian restaurant in Palm Springs' Uptown Design District.
frontmatter.agent_summary: Workshop Kitchen + Bar is a Palm Springs modern Californian restaurant opened September 2012, recognized by the James Beard Foundation for restaurant design and listed in the Michelin Guide (Plate 2024, 2025), known for wood-fired plates, cocktails, and a cast-concrete dining room.

seeds.what_it_is: Workshop Kitchen + Bar is a modern Californian restaurant on North Palm Canyon Drive in Palm Springs, opened September 22, 2012 by chef-owner Michael Beckman and co-owner Joseph Mourani. The cast-concrete dining room by SOMA Architects won the 2015 James Beard Award for Outstanding Restaurant Design (75+ seats). The menu centers on wood-fired plates, handmade pastas, and a cocktail program that has anchored Uptown Design District dining for over a decade.
key_facts: Opened September 22, 2012 (not October as some sources state)
2015 James Beard Award — Outstanding Restaurant Design, 75+ seats (SOMA Architects)
2013 Restaurant & Bar Design Awards — Best Design for the Americas
Michelin Guide California — Plate 2024 and 2025 (multi-year recommended; '4-time' specific tally [UNVERIFIED])
Yelp 1,596 reviews; OpenTable 1,788 ratings [UNVERIFIED]; Tripadvisor 831 reviews [UNVERIFIED]
Original chef/owner Michael Beckman and Joseph Mourani still attached per current site

frontmatter.title: Le Vallauris
frontmatter.description: Historic French-Mediterranean fine-dining restaurant on a 1924 estate in Palm Springs.
frontmatter.agent_summary: Le Vallauris is a Palm Springs French-Mediterranean fine-dining restaurant operating since 1973 in the 1924 Roberson house, known for its ficus-lined patio, lobster ravioli, and Grand Marnier souffle; the property was acquired by investor Ron Burkle around 2020 and now sits in the Soho House restaurants portfolio.

seeds.what_it_is: Le Vallauris is a classic French-Mediterranean fine-dining restaurant in downtown Palm Springs, founded in 1973 by Paul Bruggemans in the 1924 Roberson house. The ficus-lined patio and historic interior have anchored special-occasion dining for over 50 years. The underlying property was acquired by investor Ron Burkle around 2020 (bundled with the Willows Inn and O'Donnell House) and the restaurant now appears under the Soho House restaurants portfolio. Long-tenured chef Jean-Paul Lair appears to have departed.
key_facts: Founded 1973 by Paul Bruggemans in the 1924 Roberson house (not 1927)
OpenTable Top 100 Best Outdoor Dining Restaurants in America 2023 (2022 inclusion not confirmed)
Property acquired by Ron Burkle ~2020; now in Soho House restaurants portfolio (operational vs. branded role ambiguous)
Yelp 800+ reviews; Tripadvisor ~1,100; OpenTable 5,000+
Long-time chef Jean-Paul Lair (~29 years) shown as former on LinkedIn — current chef [UNVERIFIED]
AAA Four Diamond and Zagat ratings are legacy/marketing references; not independently confirmed as current
flags: Current operational ownership (Soho House vs. portfolio) ambiguous

frontmatter.title: Cheeky's
frontmatter.description: Creative breakfast and brunch spot in Palm Springs known for its weekly-changing menu and bacon flight.
frontmatter.agent_summary: Cheeky's is a Palm Springs breakfast and brunch restaurant open since 2008 under Tara Lazar / F10 Hospitality, known for a weekly-changing creative menu, the signature bacon flight, and long weekend waits; Michelin Guide recommended.

seeds.what_it_is: Cheeky's is a creative breakfast and brunch restaurant on North Palm Canyon Drive in Palm Springs, opened in 2008 by restaurateur Tara Lazar (F10 Hospitality, formerly Foundation 10 Creative). The menu changes weekly and is anchored by signature items including the bacon flight, French toast, chicken & waffles, and house cinnamon rolls. Long waits, especially on weekends, are part of the experience.
key_facts: Opened 2008 under Tara Lazar / F10 Hospitality; ownership stable (opening month not pinned)
Michelin Guide California — recommended (not starred)
Tripadvisor #27 of 309 Palm Springs restaurants (~1,499 reviews, 4.3/5)
Yelp 3,400+ reviews
Owner Tara Lazar featured in Palm Springs Life 'Women Who Lead' 2022
Press coverage in LA Magazine, Food GPS

frontmatter.title: Sherman's Deli & Bakery (Palm Springs)
frontmatter.description: Historic New York-style Jewish deli and bakery in downtown Palm Springs, family-owned since 1963.
frontmatter.agent_summary: Sherman's Deli & Bakery is a Palm Springs New York-style Jewish deli operating since 1963, founded by Sherman Harris and now run by his children Sam and Janet, known for pastrami, corned beef, matzo ball soup, and bakery items; featured on Food Network's Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives.

seeds.what_it_is: Sherman's Deli & Bakery is a New York-style Jewish delicatessen and bakery in downtown Palm Springs, founded in 1963 by Sherman Harris. The Harris family arrived in Palm Springs in 1953 and worked at Laury's Steak Ranch before opening Sherman's. The deli is still family-run by Harris's children Sam and Janet. A second location operates in Palm Desert.
key_facts: Founded 1963; 60+ years continuously family-owned (Harris family arrived in Palm Springs 1953)
Featured on Food Network's Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice 2025
Yelp 4,127 reviews (Palm Springs location); brand-wide footprint 6,000+ across platforms
Listed in Visit Palm Springs historic / long-operating restaurants coverage
Recurring critique: price creep (e.g., reports of $25 bagel-and-lox)

frontmatter.title: Sherman's Deli & Bakery (Palm Desert)
frontmatter.description: Palm Desert outpost of the family-owned Sherman's Jewish deli, brand operating since 1963.
frontmatter.agent_summary: Sherman's Deli & Bakery Palm Desert is the Highway 111 sibling of the historic 1963 Palm Springs deli, family-owned by the Harris children, known for pastrami, house-baked rye, and corned beef hash; featured on Food Network's Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives.

seeds.what_it_is: Sherman's Deli & Bakery in Palm Desert is the sibling location to the historic 1963 Palm Springs original, operated by the Harris family. The Palm Desert outpost was the site of the Food Network Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives feature with Guy Fieri (per Tripadvisor and Palm Springs Life attribution; some episode trackers credit the Palm Springs location). The menu mirrors the original — pastrami, corned beef, breakfast classics, and house-baked bread and pastries.
key_facts: Sister to the 1963 Palm Springs original; same Harris-family ownership
Featured on Food Network's Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives (DDD attribution split between PD and PS in trackers)
Yelp ~2,369 reviews; Tripadvisor ~1,672 reviews
KESQ Best of Coachella Valley — Best Desserts winner
Recurring sentiment: positive on pastrami and breakfast, pricing-driven mixed undercurrent
Palm Springs Life feature ('Family Ties')

frontmatter.title: Morgan's in the Desert
frontmatter.description: Signature contemporary American restaurant at La Quinta Resort & Club, Curio Collection by Hilton.
frontmatter.agent_summary: Morgan's in the Desert is the signature contemporary-American restaurant at La Quinta Resort & Club (Curio Collection by Hilton), named for resort founder Walter H. Morgan, known for farm-to-table sourcing, piano-bar patio, and a resort dining heritage that traces to the 1926 La Quinta Hotel.

seeds.what_it_is: Morgan's in the Desert is the signature dining venue at La Quinta Resort & Club (now part of Hilton's Curio Collection), a property dating to 1926. Named for resort founder Walter H. Morgan, the restaurant emphasizes Coachella Valley regional sourcing, seasonal menus, and a piano-bar patio. Leadership has shifted multiple times since 3x James Beard Award-winning chef Jimmy Schmidt departed in 2016.
key_facts: Anchored at La Quinta Resort, a property dating to 1926
Previously led by 3x James Beard Award-winning chef Jimmy Schmidt (departed November 2016)
Current Morgan's-specific executive chef unconfirmed; Jos van Hengel named Chef de Cuisine in 2021; Andrew Cooper referenced at resort level [UNVERIFIED current]
Yelp 552; TripAdvisor 536; Restaurantji 623 — aggregate ~1,500-2,000
Hosted James Beard Foundation Celebrity Chef Tour dinners (historical)
Resort now Curio Collection by Hilton; rebranded from Waldorf Astoria in Nov 2021

frontmatter.title: Copley's on Palm Canyon
frontmatter.description: Contemporary American restaurant in Palm Springs on the historic Cary Grant estate.
frontmatter.agent_summary: Copley's on Palm Canyon is a Palm Springs contemporary-American restaurant open since December 2004 on the former Cary Grant estate, owned by chef Andrew Manion Copley, Juliana Manion Copley, and Greg Butterfield, known for the patio, salmon, and OpenTable romantic-restaurants recognition.

seeds.what_it_is: Copley's on Palm Canyon is a contemporary American restaurant in Palm Springs that opened in December 2004 on the grounds of the historic Cary Grant estate. The restaurant is owned and operated by chef Andrew Manion Copley, Juliana Manion Copley, and Greg Butterfield. Juliana co-founded Palm Springs Restaurant Week and serves as president of the Palm Springs Desert Resorts Restaurant Association.
key_facts: Opened December 2004; same owner-operator team for 21 years
Located on the former Cary Grant estate
OpenTable 100 Most Romantic Restaurants in America (2024)
Tripadvisor #14 of 309 Palm Springs restaurants (was #12 in older snapshots); OpenTable 7,585 reviews
Palm Springs Desert Resort Communities CVA Restaurateurs of the Year 2009
Co-owner Juliana Copley: co-founder of Palm Springs Restaurant Week

frontmatter.title: Bar Cecil
frontmatter.description: French-leaning American brasserie and cocktail bar in Palm Springs, frequently called the city's hardest reservation.
frontmatter.agent_summary: Bar Cecil is a Palm Springs French-leaning American brasserie and cocktail bar opened in 2021 by John Janulis with Sparrows Lodge / Holiday House partners, Michelin Guide recommended and featured on OpenTable's 2025 Top 100 Restaurants in America, known for oysters, steak tartare, pork chop, and a $50 caviar martini.

seeds.what_it_is: Bar Cecil is a French-leaning American brasserie and cocktail bar in Palm Springs that opened in 2021. Lead partner John Janulis works with majority owners Richard Crisman and Jeff Brock of Sparrows Lodge and Holiday House. The room — designed with maximalist art and brasserie banquettes — has become one of the most sought-after reservations in the city, with bar walk-in seating widely used as a workaround. Adjacent lounge Beaton's expanded the footprint.
key_facts: Opened 2021; ~5 years operating with stable ownership
Michelin Guide California — Plate 2024 and 2025
OpenTable Top 100 Restaurants in America 2025 (4.8 stars from 587 reviews)
The Infatuation: named favorite date-night spot; included in 25 Best Palm Springs Restaurants & Bars
Time Out feature (May 2025): 'the hardest reservation in Palm Springs'
Yelp 547 reviews (4.5 stars); OpenTable 1,858 reviews

frontmatter.title: Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey
frontmatter.description: Chef-driven sushi and Japanese whiskey bar in Palm Springs from Engin Onural.
frontmatter.agent_summary: Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey is a Palm Springs sushi and Japanese-whiskey bar opened in 2018 by chef Engin Onural (Onural Group, also The Venue in Palm Desert), known for creative rolls, ahi tuna sashimi, Lobster Zucchini Blossoms, and a Japanese-government sushi certification held by the chef.

seeds.what_it_is: Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey is a chef-driven sushi restaurant and Japanese whiskey bar in Palm Springs, opened in 2018 by Engin Onural under the Onural Group. Onural has operated in the Coachella Valley since 2011 with The Venue in Palm Desert. The restaurant is known for creative rolls, omakase-leaning service at the bar, and a curated Japanese whiskey list. Critique themes are price-to-portion ratio and limited nigiri selection.
key_facts: Opened 2018; ~8 years operating; stable single-owner (Onural Group)

seeds.what_it_is: El Tranvia is a family Mexican restaurant in the City of Coachella on 6th Street, operated by Oscar Ventura with recipes traced to his grandparents' pushcart-to-food-wagon (the original 'tranvia') in Zamora, Michoacán in the 1960s. The current Coachella storefront, per the owner's Palm Springs Life interview, opened roughly 15 years ago (~2010-2011) and moved to its current 6th Street location about 10 years ago. The 1969 date that circulates in third-party copy refers to the family pushcart in Mexico, not the Coachella restaurant.

The restaurant held Wine Spectator's Best of Award of Excellence every year from 2005 onward.

The restaurant is consistently among OpenTable's most-booked in Palm Springs.

seeds.what_it_is: Stuft Pizza Bar & Grill is a family-owned pizza and American bar & grill in Old Town La Quinta. The parent brand was founded in 1976 in Cerritos by Jack and Pat Bertram; the La Quinta location has been operating since 2004, with son John Bertram running desert operations and patriarch Jack still visiting. The format leans family-casual, with a dog-friendly patio and a happy-hour-driven crowd.
key_facts: Parent brand founded 1976 in Cerritos by Jack and Pat Bertram
La Quinta location operating since 2004 (~22 years)
Tripadvisor #3 of 146 La Quinta restaurants; 1,065 reviews (4.3)
Yelp 1,300+ reviews
Featured in Old Town La Quinta official dining guide and Visit Greater Palm Springs directory
Coachella Valley Weekly legacy/anniversary coverage (Dec 2023)
flags: 'Wine Spectator award-winning wine list' is self-claimed; not found in Wine Spectator's public award database

seeds.what_it_is: Ernie's Bar & Grill is an American gastropub and post-golf clubhouse grill at the PGA West Tournament Clubhouse in La Quinta. The clubhouse traces to the Stadium Course era (course opened 1986), and the restaurant is named for co-developer Ernie Vossler (d. 2013). The format leans patio-with-mountain-views, breakfast/post-golf service, and group dining. Recurring critiques cluster on service consistency at peak times.

Stadium Course opened 1986; exact restaurant naming/opening year [UNVERIFIED]

seeds.what_it_is: Jalisco Restaurant is a family-owned Jalisco-style Mexican restaurant in the City of Coachella, operating at 1605 6th Street since 1980. frontmatter.title: El Mexicali Cafe
frontmatter.description: Multi-generational Indio Mexican restaurant founded by Carmen Murguia in 1981, with sibling El Mexicali Cafe II named to USA Today Restaurants of the Year 2026.
frontmatter.agent_summary: El Mexicali Cafe is an Indio Mexican family restaurant founded in 1981 by Carmen Murguia and run by daughters Monica (original) and Laura (II), known for signature chiles gueritos (shrimp-stuffed yellow peppers); sibling location El Mexicali Cafe II named to USA Today Restaurants of the Year 2026.

seeds.what_it_is: El Mexicali Cafe is a multi-generational Indio Mexican restaurant founded in 1981 by Carmen Murguia, with the original at 82720 Indio Blvd and a sibling 'II' location. The signature dish — chiles gueritos (shrimp-stuffed yellow peppers with mayo and soy) — is the defining draw, alongside family-recipe seafood and the railroad-adjacent ambiance. The City of Indio recognized the restaurant's 40th anniversary in March 2021; El Mexicali Cafe II was named to USA Today Restaurants of the Year on February 11, 2026.
key_facts: Founded 1981 by Carmen Murguia; 45+ years continuous operation; family-run by daughters Monica and Laura
USA Today Restaurants of the Year 2026 — awarded to El Mexicali Cafe II sibling location (Feb 11, 2026)
City of Indio 40th anniversary proclamation (2021)
Certificate of recognition from State Senator Melissa Melendez (2021)
Yelp ~415 (original) + ~601 (II); combined Yelp 1000+ across family
Listed by Visit Greater Palm Springs

frontmatter.title: Shields Date Garden
frontmatter.description: Century-old Indio date garden, gift shop, and on-site Cafe at Shields with signature date shake.
frontmatter.agent_summary: Shields Date Garden is a 17-acre Indio date garden, gift shop, and on-site Cafe at Shields, operating since 1924 (current Highway 111 site since 1953) with the cafe opened in 2010; recognized by Coachella Valley Independent as perennial Best Date Shake winner.
frontmatter.category: California cuisine cafe / date garden destination

seeds.what_it_is: Shields Date Garden is a 17-acre date grove, retail shop, and on-site cafe in Indio. The date garden has been operating since 1924 (founders Floyd and Bess Shields), with the current Highway 111 site since 1953 and the Cafe at Shields opening in 2010 (sold to Jewel Date Company in 2005). The cafe serves California cuisine breakfast and lunch with date-forward signature items and Mexican-influenced plates. The date shake is the most consistent draw.
key_facts: Date garden since 1924; current Hwy 111 site since 1953; sold to Jewel Date Company 2005; Cafe at Shields opened 2010
Coachella Valley Independent Best of Coachella Valley readers' poll — Best Date Shake (perennial, 10+ years per 2023 CV Independent feature)
Tripadvisor ranked #3 of 133 restaurants in Indio (Cafe at Shields)
Recognized by City of Indio, County of Riverside, and State of California (April 2022) as the official county date shake creator
Featured by Palm Springs Life, Atlas Obscura, Coachella Valley Weekly
Yelp ~1,000+ on main listing; ~400+ on cafe listing

frontmatter.title: Nobu Restaurant (Indian Wells)
frontmatter.description: Permanent Indian Wells outpost of the Nobu Japanese-Peruvian global brand.
frontmatter.agent_summary: Nobu Restaurant Indian Wells is the permanent Japanese-Peruvian outpost of the Nobu Matsuhisa global brand inside the Indian Wells Tennis Garden, originated as a 2014 BNP Paribas Open pop-up and made permanent in February 2023, known for yellowtail jalapeño, black cod miso, and rock shrimp tempura.

seeds.what_it_is: Nobu Restaurant Indian Wells is the permanent Coachella Valley outpost of the Nobu Matsuhisa global brand. Originally a 2014 BNP Paribas Open pop-up at the Indian Wells Tennis Garden, it became a permanent fixture in February 2023. The menu pulls from the global Nobu canon — yellowtail jalapeño, black cod miso, rock shrimp tempura — and the Tanoshi Hour expands accessibility. Chef Nobu Matsuhisa's brand-level credentials include a James Beard Best New Restaurant win in 1995 and Who's Who of Food & Beverage in America in 2002.
key_facts: Originated as 2014 BNP Paribas Open pop-up; permanent fixture since February 2023
At the Indian Wells Tennis Garden
Yelp 148 reviews; OpenTable 4.7 from 771 diners; Restaurant Guru aggregates ~974
Chef Nobu Matsuhisa — James Beard Best New Restaurant 1995; Who's Who of Food & Beverage in America 2002 (brand-level credentials)
Tanoshi Hour is a recurring access point
Featured in Palm Springs Life and OpenTable Best Fine Dining Palm Springs/Palm Desert Cities list
flags: Prior 'Best California Chef 2002' Matzuhisa credential is not in JBF records — removed

frontmatter.title: Melvyn's Restaurant & Bar
frontmatter.description: Classic continental supper club at the Ingleside Estate in Palm Springs, marked 50 years in 2025.
frontmatter.agent_summary: Melvyn's Restaurant & Bar is a Palm Springs classic continental supper club at the Ingleside Estate, opened 1975 by Mel Haber, known for tableside Steak Diane, live piano in the Casablanca Lounge, and a Sinatra-era heritage; 50th anniversary marked October-November 2025.

seeds.what_it_is: Melvyn's Restaurant & Bar is a classic continental supper club at the Ingleside Estate (Ingleside Inn) in Palm Springs, opened in 1975 by Mel Haber. The room marked its 50th anniversary in October-November 2025. The format — tableside Steak Diane, Caesar, flambé desserts, and live piano in the Casablanca Lounge — preserves a Sinatra-era experience. Ownership changed after Haber's death in 2016.
key_facts: Opened 1975 by Mel Haber; 50th anniversary marked Oct-Nov 2025
Ownership changed after Haber's 2016 death

frontmatter.title: Mr. Lyons Steakhouse
frontmatter.description: Modern supper-club-style steakhouse on South Palm Canyon from Tara Lazar / F10 Hospitality.
frontmatter.agent_summary: Mr. Lyons Steakhouse is a Palm Springs supper-club-style steakhouse, reopened May 2015 by Foundation 10 Creative (Tara Lazar) inside the 1945 Lyons English Grille building, known for Beef Wellington, tableside service, and a Palm Springs Life Editors' Pick Best Steakhouse credential (2021 and 2022 per restaurant marketing).

seeds.what_it_is: Mr. Lyons Steakhouse is a modern supper-club-style steakhouse on South Palm Canyon in Palm Springs. The building operated as Lyons English Grille from 1945; Foundation 10 Creative (Tara Lazar) purchased it in 2014 and reopened as Mr. Lyons in May 2015. The dim dining room leans 1940s supper-club, with Beef Wellington and tableside service the recurring praise themes; transparent kitchen-appreciation fees and pricing are recurring critique themes.

frontmatter.title: Johannes

frontmatter.agent_summary: Johannes is a Palm Springs modern European restaurant with Austrian heritage and Asian fusion accents, opened October 1999 by chef-owner Johannes Bacher, known for Wiener Schnitzel (featured in Mimi Sheraton's 1,000 Foods to Eat Before You Die), apple strudel, and a 25-year continuous ownership.

seeds.what_it_is: Johannes is a modern European restaurant in Palm Springs with Austrian heritage and Asian fusion accents, opened in late October 1999 by chef-owner Johannes Bacher. The restaurant celebrated 25 years in 2024.
